notes: Tested whether the engineering layer contradicts, weakens, or inverts the traditional meaning. (1) Does it contradict yadṛcchayā? No — it preserves the unsought-arrival as the operative thing. (2) Does it weaken sukhinaḥ kṣatriyāḥ? No — preserves 'fortunate' as descriptive, not prescriptive. (3) Does it invert svarga-dvāram apāvṛtam? No — preserves the unobstructed-gate framing. (4) Does it import the militarism connotation? Explicitly no — the engineering layer scopes the analog narrowly to dharma-aligned role-fit problems. Reverse-translate test: a Sanskrit reader reading only the engineering layer would reconstruct a stance compatible with yadṛcchayā + dharma-yuddha + sukhinaḥ — the 'arrived unsought' marker, the 'role-aligned dharmic problem' content, and the 'lucky operator' descriptor are all reconstructable. PASS on both 3.6 and 7.10.
